tldr: Pipeline combining Template-Based Modeling and Protenix to generate diverse structural templates, which are cross-pollinated into RNAPro with RibonanzaNet2 embeddings to maximize diversity for a best-of-5 scoring metric.
summary: The solution targets an RNA structure prediction competition where the metric rewards the best of 5 predictions. The author generates structural diversity by first running Template-Based Modeling (TBM) on sequences >1000 nt and using Protenix in no-MSA/no-template mode on shorter sequences. These outputs are then fed as templates into RNAPro, which refines structures using RibonanzaNet2 embeddings, 10 recycling cycles, and 200 diffusion steps. The key score driver was cross-pollinating Protenix predictions as templates into RNAPro to ensure independent structural hypotheses, alongside retaining raw Protenix outputs to prevent overcorrection.
extensive_summary:
## Pipeline Overview
The competition metric scores the best of 5 predictions, prioritizing diversity alongside accuracy. The pipeline splits by sequence length and generates 5 diverse predictions via template cross-pollination.

## Phase 1 — Template-Based Modeling (TBM)
TBM generates fast, diverse templates. Alignment uses BioPython's `PairwiseAligner` (global mode, gap penalties `open: -8, extend: -0.4`). C1' coordinates map to query residues, with gaps filled via linear interpolation or 3Å extrapolation. Geometry refinement applies `adaptive_rna_constraints` per chain segment: i↔i+1 bonds (~5.95 Å), i↔i+2 soft angles (~10.20 Å), Laplacian smoothing, and steric self-avoidance for chains ≥25 residues. Correction strength scales with `(1 - similarity)`. Five variants are produced: untouched template, mild Gaussian noise scaled by similarity, hinge rotation on the longest segment, independent rigid-body jitter, and low-frequency wiggles via control points.

## Phase 2 — Protenix
Protenix runs in no-MSA, no-template mode on sequences ≤1000 nt (truncated to 512 nt for featurization). Five samples are generated; the first two are extracted via `centre_atom_mask` (fallback to `atom_to_tokatom_idx`) and forwarded as templates.

## Phase 3 — RNAPro
RNAPro refines structures using RibonanzaNet2 embeddings, 10 recycling cycles, and 200 diffusion steps. Four slots condition on different templates (TBM 1-2, Protenix 1-2). The fifth prediction is raw Protenix[0], retained for targets with close training analogs where RNAPro overcorrects.

## Key Findings & Lessons
Cross-pollinating Protenix outputs into RNAPro was critical, as differing inductive biases explore independent structure space. Raw Protenix predictions prevented overcorrection. Conversely, Protenix with MSA added cost without benefit, >2 TBM template slots plateaued performance, and coordinate ensemble averaging underperformed the best-of-5 strategy. All runs used a single Kaggle P100 GPU.
preprocessing: Sequence truncation to 512 nt for Protenix featurization
Gap filling via linear interpolation and 3Å extrapolation
ensemble: Best-of-5 selection from a pool of 5 structurally diverse predictions generated via TBM templates, Protenix templates fed into RNAPro, and raw Protenix.
what_worked: Feeding Protenix outputs into RNAPro as templates to explore independent structure space
Retaining raw Protenix predictions to prevent RNAPro overcorrection
what_did_not_work: Using more than 2 TBM template slots in RNAPro
